title: Sustainability, hosting and reviewer-rotation/withdrawal-handling plan
context: For the corpus to have a life beyond M3, there must be a documented plan for: who maintains it after delivery; how consent withdrawals are processed; how reviewer seats are rotated as the project scales; and where the audio and explorer are hosted long-term. The host-independent ID namespace (id-001) decouples identifiers from hosting, but the hosting question itself must be answered. Depends on place-name-pronunciation-partner-003.
objective: Write and commit a SUSTAINABILITY.md document covering: post-delivery maintenance responsibilities; the hosting plan for audio and the explorer (Wikimedia Commons, steward infrastructure, or object storage); the consent-withdrawal handling process; the reviewer-rotation plan for privacy/consent and cultural/linguistic reviewer seats; and the quarterly outcome-review cadence.
acceptanceCriteria: SUSTAINABILITY.md is committed covering: post-delivery maintenance responsibilities with named roles; the agreed hosting arrangement for audio and the static explorer; the consent-withdrawal handling process (removal from corpus and future exports, honest caveat on third-party copies).

Reviewer-rotation plan defines: how privacy/consent and cultural/linguistic reviewer seats are filled when current reviewers rotate out; minimum seat-count requirements; escalation if a seat is empty.
Quarterly outcome-review cadence is documented with reference to the metrics defined in metrics-001.
Hosting plan names the actual host (or acknowledges TO BE CONFIRMED if still pending) and records the archival strategy (mirrors, formats retained).
Maintainer reviews and approves SUSTAINABILITY.md before the task is marked done.
output: SUSTAINABILITY.md committed via PR covering maintenance, hosting, withdrawal handling, reviewer rotation, and quarterly review cadence.
